text: In a p-n-p transistor working as a common-base amplifier, when the current gain is 0.96 and emitter current is 7.2 mA, the base current is
explanation:
Current gain in common-base: \(\alpha = I_C/I_E\)

\(I_C = \alpha\times I_E = 0.96\times7.2 = 6.912\text{ mA}\)

\(I_B = I_E - I_C = 7.2 - 6.912 = 0.288\approx0.29\text{ mA}\)
name: Common-Base Transistor Base Current

text: In a photodiode, the value of the emf produced by a monochromatic light beam is directly proportional to
options: The barrier potential at the p-n junction
The intensity of light falling on the photodiode
The frequency of light falling on the photodiode
The voltage applied at the p-n junction
explanation: In a photodiode, more photons (higher intensity) generate more electron-hole pairs, increasing the photocurrent and hence the emf. The emf is directly proportional to the **intensity** of incident light.
name: Photodiode EMF Dependence

text: Assuming the junction diode is ideal, the current in the arrangement (diode with 100 Ω resistor, 3 V and 1 V sources) is
explanation:
The ideal diode is forward biased and acts as short circuit.

Net voltage across resistor \(= 3 - 1 = 2\text{ V}\)

\(I = V/R = 2/100 = 0.02\text{ A} = 20\text{ mA}\)
name: Ideal Diode Circuit Current

text: A Zener diode is made by
options: Heavily doping both p and n sides of a p-n junction
Heavily doping p-side and lightly doping n-side
Lightly doping p-side and heavily doping n-side
Lightly doping both p and n sides
explanation: A Zener diode is made by **heavily doping both p and n sides**. This creates a very thin depletion region (\(<10^{-6}\) m), leading to an extremely high electric field even under small reverse bias — enabling Zener breakdown.
name: Zener Diode Construction

text: In the circuit with two ideal diodes and resistors (6 Ω, 4 Ω, 2 Ω, 8 Ω, 12 Ω), with −10 V at A and −2 V at B, the equivalent resistance between A and B is
explanation:
Both diodes D1 and D2 are reverse biased (open circuit) because the potential at A is more negative than at B.

The remaining path has resistors 8 Ω, 2 Ω, and 6 Ω in series:

\(R_{AB} = 8 + 2 + 6 = 16\text{ Ω}\)
name: Diode Circuit Equivalent Resistance

text: In a p-type semiconductor, the donor level is at 50 meV above the valence band. The maximum wavelength of light photon required to produce one electron is (\(h=6.6\times10^{-34}\) Js, \(c=3\times10^8\) m/s)
name: Maximum Wavelength for Semiconductor Excitation

text: In a transistor, \(\alpha\) varies between 20/21 and 100/101. Then \(\beta\) varies between
name: Transistor α to β Conversion

text: For an LED to emit light in the visible region, the energy band gap should be in the range (\(h=6.6\times10^{-34}\) Js, \(c=3\times10^8\) m/s)
explanation:
Visible light frequency range: \(4\times10^{14}\) Hz to \(7\times10^{14}\) Hz.

\(E_{min} = hf_{min} = \dfrac{6.6\times10^{-34}\times4\times10^{14}}{1.6\times10^{-19}}\approx1.65\approx1.7\text{ eV}\)

\(E_{max} = hf_{max}\approx2.88\approx3\text{ eV}\)

So band gap should be **1.7 eV to 3.1 eV**.
name: LED Band Gap for Visible Light

text: In a common-emitter circuit with \(\beta=100\) and \(V_{CE}=7\) V (\(V_{BE}\approx0\)), if the collector load is 2 kΩ and \(V_{CC}=15\) V, the base current is
explanation:
KVL: \(-15 + 2000\cdot I_C + 7 = 0\implies I_C = 4\text{ mA}\)

\(I_B = I_C/\beta = 4\times10^{-3}/100 = 0.04\text{ mA}\)
name: Common-Emitter Base Current

text: Three amplifiers with voltage gains 10, 20 and 30 are connected in series. If the input signal has a peak value of 1 mV, the peak output voltage is
explanation:
Total gain \(A = A_1\times A_2\times A_3 = 10\times20\times30 = 6000\)

\(V_{out} = A\times V_{in} = 6000\times1\times10^{-3} = 6\text{ V}\)
name: Cascaded Amplifiers Output

text: In a Zener diode voltage regulator circuit (\(R_1=500\text{ Ω}\), \(R_2=1500\text{ Ω}\), \(V_Z=10\text{ V}\), supply 15 V), the current through the Zener diode is
explanation:
Total current: \(I = (15-10)/500 = 5/500 = 10\text{ mA}\)

Current through \(R_2\): \(I_2 = 10/1500 = 6.67\text{ mA}\)

Zener current: \(I_Z = I - I_2 = 10 - 6.67 = 3.33\text{ mA}\)
name: Zener Diode Current in Voltage Regulator

text: For the logic circuit shown (two inputs A=1, B=1 through NAND/OR combination), the values of \(Y_1\) and \(Y_2\) respectively are
explanation:
With A=1 and B=1:

First OR gate: output \(P = A+B = 1\)

NAND gate (inputs P=1, B=1): \(Y_1 = \overline{1\cdot1} = 0\)

Second OR gate (inputs 1, \(Y_1=0\)): \(Y_2 = 1+0 = 1\)

So \(Y_1=0\), \(Y_2=1\).
name: Logic Gate Output Evaluation

text: In a circuit with ideal diodes and resistors (3 Ω, 2 Ω series branches with 20 V supply), the current through the cell is
explanation:
One diode is reverse biased (open), one is forward biased (acts as short circuit).

Effective resistance: \(R = 3 + 2 + 3 + 2 = 10\text{ Ω}\)

\(I = V/R = 20/10 = 2\text{ A}\)
name: Ideal Diode Circuit with Multiple Components

text: In an n-p-n common-emitter transistor circuit (\(V_{CC}=8\) V, \(I_C=5\) mA, \(V_{BE}=0.6\) V, \(V_{CE}=3\) V, \(\beta=50\)), the values of \(R_1\) and \(R_2\) respectively are
name: Transistor Biasing Resistors

text: The two combinations of NAND gates in figures (i) and (ii) are equivalent to
explanation:
Circuit (i): Two inputs into NAND then complement: \(\overline{\overline{A}\cdot\overline{B}}=A+B\) — **OR gate**.

Circuit (ii): Both inputs into NAND then NAND again: \(\overline{\overline{AB}\cdot\overline{AB}}=AB\) — **AND gate**.
name: NAND Gate Combinations

text: For the combination of logic gates shown (A and B into two OR gates then NAND), the equivalent logic gate is
explanation:
\(Y_1 = A+B\), \(Y_2 = A+B\)

NAND output: \(Y = \overline{Y_1\cdot Y_2} = \overline{(A+B)(A+B)} = \overline{A+B}\)

This is equivalent to a **NOR gate**.
name: Logic Gate Equivalence

text: In common-base configuration, a transistor has current amplification \(\alpha=0.95\). In common-emitter configuration, if base current changes by 2 µA, the change in collector current is
name: Common-Emitter Collector Current Change

text: Match the following: SSI → logic gates ≤ 10; MSI → logic gates < 100; LSI → logic gates < 1000; VLSI → logic gates > 1000. The correct match is
explanation:
SSI (Small Scale Integration): logic gates ≤ 10 → **III**

MSI (Medium Scale Integration): logic gates < 100 → **I**

LSI (Large Scale Integration): logic gates < 1000 → **IV**

VLSI (Very Large Scale Integration): logic gates > 1000 → **II**

Correct: A-III, B-I, C-IV, D-II
name: IC Integration Levels Classification
